title: ADULT PROTECTIVE SERVICES - 211info
text: COMMUNITY LIVING CASE MANAGEMENT DOUGLAS COUNTY OFFICE ADULT PROTECTIVE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Investigates allegations of abuse due to physical harm or injury, financial exploitation, verbal or emotional abuse, involuntary seclusion, wrongful restraint, unwanted sexual contact, abandonment by the caregiver, failure to provide basic care or self-neglect (when individuals lack the cognitive ability to care for themselves, which can lead to harm). 2550 NW Edenbower Boulevard Suite 104 Roseburg, OR 97471 Eligibility: Age 18 and older with intellectual and developmental disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm. Statewide abuse reporting hotline voicemail available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week. (541) 315-4300 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 315-4300 Hotline (855) 503-7233 Statewide Abuse Reporting INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call to make a report or for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Douglas County Services: Adult Protective Intervention/Investigation Elder/Dependent Adult Abuse Reporting Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - FOSTER CARE REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. COMMUNITY LIVING CASE MANAGEMENT DOUGLAS COUNTY OFFICE
